evidence: get_dhcp_config (R2): dhcp pool R2_LAN for 172.16.0.0/24 ... default-router 192.168.0.1
running-config facts: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address 172.16.0.1 mask 255.255.255.0 (shows the correct on-box gateway should be 172.16.0.1)
R2 DHCP excluded-address list: low-address 172.16.0.1 high-address 172.16.0.10 (indicates .12 is inside the assignable DHCP range)

missing_info: Where were the 25 pings originated from (source IP / device)?
Is 172.16.0.12 currently assigned and up on the LAN? Provide R2 outputs: 'show ip dhcp binding 172.16.0.12' and 'show arp | include 172.16.0.12'.
Provide the host's IP configuration for 172.16.0.12 (especially the configured/default gateway) if available.

evidence: get_dhcp_config:router2 shows 'dhcp pool R2_LAN for 172.16.0.0/24, default-router 192.168.0.1, dns 8.8.8.8, excluded 172.16.0.1-172.16.0.10'.
get_running_config:router2 shows GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address 172.16.0.1 mask 255.255.255.0 (R2's LAN IP, expected default gateway).
facts include excluded-address range '172.16.0.1-172.16.0.10' for R2, indicating 172.16.0.1 is the intended local gateway.
previous_changes: a create_dhcp_pool call for R2 specified default_router 172.16.0.1 (successful), indicating the desired setting differs from the currently observed pool.

Alert description (authoritative): 'R2's DHCP pool for 172.16.0.0/24 is configured with the wrong default gateway (192.168.0.1), causing host 172.16.0.12 to receive an incorrect gateway and be unreachable.'
Mismatch between DHCP pool default-router (192.168.0.1) and R2 LAN IP (172.16.0.1) from device configs supports that leases distributed will contain an out-of-subnet gateway.

plan_history.intent_description: R2's DHCP pool for 172.16.0.0/24 is configured with the wrong default gateway (192.168.0.1), causing host 172.16.0.12 to receive an incorrect gateway and be unreachable; fix DHCP default-router on R2 and force DHCP renewal so the host can regain connectivity.
plan_history.problem: R2 DHCP pool 'R2_LAN' has default-router set to 192.168.0.1 (R1) instead of the on-subnet gateway 172.16.0.1, causing DHCP clients in 172.16.0.0/24 (including .12) to get an invalid gateway and lose connectivity.
plan_history.fix_summary: Update R2 DHCP pool 'R2_LAN' default-router to 172.16.0.1 and clear the DHCP binding for 172.16.0.12 to force the host to obtain the corrected gateway. Operational risk: DHCP lease change may momentarily interrupt network access for affected LAN clients.

plan_history.intent_description: R2's DHCP pool R2_LAN is assigning an out-of-subnet default gateway (192.168.0.1) to hosts on 172.16.0.0/24; update the DHCP pool to use the LAN gateway 172.16.0.1 so hosts (e.g., 172.16.0.12) receive the correct default-router.
plan_history.problem: DHCP pool 'R2_LAN' on router2 has default-router 192.168.0.1 which is not the LAN gateway (172.16.0.1), causing hosts in 172.16.0.0/24 to receive an incorrect gateway and become unreachable.
plan_history.fix_summary: Set DHCP pool 'R2_LAN' default-router to 172.16.0.1 on router2 (preserve existing network, mask, DNS, and excluded range).
